label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. So, Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. Wait, February's last day is usually the 28th, except in leap years when it's the 29th. But 2001 isn't a leap year, right? Leap years are divisible by 4, but 2001 divided by 4 is 500.25, so no. So Jane was born on 2/28/2001. Today is her 16th birthday. So we need to figure out what today's date is. Since she was born on 2/28/2001, her 16th birthday would be 16 years later. Let me add 16 to 2001: 2001 + 16 = 2017. Wait, but wait—is today 2/28/2017? But wait, if today is her 16th birthday, then today is 2/28/2017. Because each birthday is the anniversary of your birth date. So today is 2/28/2017. Now the question is asking: What is the date one year ago from today? So if today is 2/28/2017, then one year ago would be 2/28/2016. But wait, 2016 was a leap year, right?
